data.title: HP Enterprise results, guidance show it's benefiting from AI momentum: analysts
data.description:
The latest quarterly results and guidance from HP Enterprise (NYSE:HPE) show the Antonio Neri-led company is still seeing momentum in artificial intelligence, Wall Street firms said.

Shares rose 2.5% in premarket trading, while competitors Dell (DELL) and Super Micro Computer (SMCI) were also higher.

Citi upgraded HP Enterprise to Buy from Neutral and raised its price target on the stock (to $26 from $23) on the belief that the company will benefit from improving demand in server and enterprise networking, and expanding AI opportunities.

"While AI orders/revenues can be lumpy, we see the potential for stronger contribution from enterprise AI and sovereigns, which bodes positively for revenue momentum and margins ahead," Citi analyst Asiya Merchant wrote in a note to clients. "Coupled with improving demand outlook in core enterprise infrastructure spending and potential EPS accretion from Juniper acquisition with management confident in closing the deal by early 2025, we are increasingly more constructive."

Deutsche Bank analyst Matt Niknam was a bit more tepid in his praise, as he said the results and guidance were an "incremental positive in the context of current valuation."

HP Enterprise's server business is seeing momentum due to both AI and traditional servers, though AI servers are negatively impacting gross margins, as the metric fell to 30.9%, its lowest level since the fourth quarter of 2020, Niknam, who has a Hold rating and $22 price target, said.

Additionally, the company's Hybrid Cloud segment provided the "bulk of the upside surprise this quarter," while Intelligent Edge (its networking business) is relatively more stable after a long period of inventory digestion at customers.

On the negative side, the forward guidance has a sequential decline in AI server sales, due in part to impacts from Nvidia's (NVDA) Blackwell delays, and "de-booking" to the tune of roughly $400M, Niknam added.

"While the F1Q outlook effectively matches consensus pre-print, we nonetheless view results as 'good enough,' in the context of a fairly discounted valuation (10x CY26E EPS) relative to HPE's near-term growth prospects," he wrote.

data.title: Dividend Roundup: Disney, Microsoft, Dell Technologies, Alphabet, and more
data.description:
This week's dividend activity included increased payouts from Disney (NYSE:DIS) and Deere (DE) as well as declarations from companies such as Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT) and Dell Technologies (NYSE:DELL).

Additionally, looking towards next week, industry players such as Alphabet (NASDAQ:GOOG) (GOOGL) and FedEx (FDX) will see the ex-dividend dates for their upcoming dividend payments.

See a breakdown below of some of the highlights of this week, along with some upcoming activity on the dividend front.

Dividend Increases

Here are some companies that have announced a dividend increase in the past week:

Aflac (AFL)

  • Dividend declared: December 2
  • New dividend amount: $0.58
  • Previous amount: $0.50
  • Increase: 16%

Deere (DE)

  • Dividend declared: December 3
  • New dividend amount: $1.62
  • Previous amount: $1.47
  • Increase: 10.2%

Disney (DIS)

  • Dividend declared: December 4
  • New dividend amount: $0.50
  • Previous amount: $0.45
  • Increase: 11.1%

Dividend Declarations

Here are some companies that have announced a dividend in the past week:

Microsoft (MSFT)

  • Dividend declared: December 3
  • Dividend amount: $0.93

Dell Technologies (DELL)

  • Dividend declared: December 3
  • Dividend amount: $0.445

Verizon (VZ)

  • Dividend declared: December 5
  • Dividend amount: $0.6775

Salesforce (CRM)

  • Dividend declared: December 5
  • Dividend amount: $0.40

Upcoming Dividend Calendar

Here are some companies that have an ex-dividend date coming next week:

Alphabet (GOOG) (GOOGL)

  • Ex-dividend date: December 9
  • Payout date: December 16

FedEx (FDX)

  • Ex-dividend date: November 26
  • Payout date: December 26

Ross Stores (ROST)

  • Ex-dividend date: December 10
  • Payout date: December 31

Devon Energy (DVN)

  • Ex-dividend date: December 13
  • Payout date: December 31

data.title: Stellantis CFO gives assurances company is moving in the right direction
data.description:
Stellantis (NYSE:STLA) shares are getting a lift Friday morning thanks to encouraging remarks from company executives at a roadshow organized by analysts at French banking firm, Oddo BHF.

According to media reports, Stellantis (STLA) CFO Doug Ostermann assured analysts that the company is equipped to handle the challenges it faces and is confident in the company’s performance in 2025 and beyond.

“The CFO expressed confidence in the automaker's ability to rebound and improve its performance next year, with, in our opinion, a seasonality in favor of [the second half] partly linked to launches,” said Oddo BHF analyst Michael Foundoukidis, adding that the changes at the head of Stellantis should enable the process of overhaul to be accelerated and intensified in the coming weeks, pending the appointment of the future CEO.

During a visit to the Maserati factory in Modena, Italy, Ostermann told employees the company is working to rebuild trust with dealers, suppliers, unions, and government officials following a “period of tension” under former CEO Carlos Tavares, according to Bloomberg.

Stellantis (STLA) shares are up 4% in premarket trading and set to extend a three-day winning streak to a fourth day.

data.title: BofA upgrades Molson Coors, expects U.S. beer industry volumes to improve in 2025
data.description:
BofA on Friday upgraded Molson Coors Beverage (NYSE:TAP) to Buy from Neutral and raised its price objective from $57 to $70, expecting 2025 to be a 'more normal year' given that the beer industry has fully digested the Bud Light controversy.

Shares of Molson Coors (TAP) rose +2.53% to $62.50 in premarket trade, having shed about 1.7% in value in the past one year.

The U.S. beer industry should perform more in line with historical levels after a challenging 2024, BofA said, adding that TAP faces easy year-ago comparisons and no headwind related to the termination of the Pabst contract brewing agreement.

"We expect cost inflation to be manageable despite the prospect for higher aluminum cost," the investment bank said.

It also expects strong free cash flow and modest debt leverage to enable TAP to continue to repurchase shares, which is reflected in BofA's above-consensus FY25 EPS estimate of $6.0 vs consensus estimate of $5.96.

Overall, if the market strength post election continues into next year and there is a melt-up in value stocks, BofA analysts view Molson Coors (TAP) as the most attractive option in its consumer staples coverage universe.

BofA's forecast assumes that TAP’s share loss improves to a 30bps decline from a 110bps decline.

Seeking Alpha analysts have a Buy rating on the company.

The top-selling beers in the U.S. include Constellation Brands' (STZ) Modelo Especial and Molson Coors' (TAP) Coors Light.
